text:
Once there was a girl named Lily who loved collecting seashells. In years gone by, her grandmother would tell her tales of mystical places where the prettiest shells could be found—a land where seashows sang songs of the sea.

Lily didn’t just collect shells; she collected memories. She’d nestle them into little booklets. When autumn came, she’d arrange them like treasure in her attic.

One day a storm blew in suddenly. Gale-force winds rattled the window panes. Through the window’s mist, Lily saw something flickering—an owl with feathers like snowflakes.

The owl hooted softly, and Lily approached slowly. It flew to her and perched on her finger. It didn’t frighten her at all. In fact, something deep inside her softened at its eyes.

“How did you find me?”

“I don’t know how to fly so well.”

“We’re the same.”

“You’re kind.”

“I’m just an owl.”

They talked
